message:
fcp-mesh/node: thread local and peer zones through build_planner_input

Fixes flywheel_connectors-fqzmp (tracked) and closes the regression
window opened by the earlier placeholder in build_planner_input where
both local and peer candidates were emitted with `zones: Vec::new()`,
so the planner's zone-policy filter could not enforce any zone-affinity
placement — every candidate was "zoneless" from the planner's view.

## What changed

- Added `local_zones: HashSet<ZoneId>` to `MeshNode`, defaulted to empty
  on construction. Empty is the "not-yet-enrolled" sentinel; the
  planner's existing `zone_policy_rejects_nodes_with_unknown_zones`
  pathway keeps handling that case conservatively.
- Added `update_local_zones(&mut self, HashSet<ZoneId>)` + a read-only
  `local_zones(&self) -> &HashSet<ZoneId>` accessor. The setter is the
  enrollment / MeshIdentity hook point after a node joins (or is
  re-enrolled into) a zone.
- `build_planner_input` now populates `zones` from `self.local_zones`
  for the local candidate and from `PeerState.zones` for each peer
  candidate. The two `TODO: populate from mesh enrollment data` /
  `TODO: populate from peer zone advertisements` stubs are removed.

## Why this was broken

The planner already had zone-policy support and the `PlannerCandidate`
struct already carried a `zones: Vec<ZoneId>` field, but the feed-in
from MeshNode hardcoded `Vec::new()`. Operators enrolling a node into
`z:work` and expecting the planner to prefer same-zone placement saw
the planner behave as if every node was zoneless and pick candidates
at random.

## Test coverage

Added `plan_execution_populates_zones_from_local_and_peer_state` which:

1. Installs a connector on both the local node and a peer.
2. Enrolls the local node into `z:work` via `update_local_zones`.
3. Enrolls the peer into `z:public` via the existing attestation-layer
   `update_peer_zones` setter.
4. Calls `plan_execution` and asserts both candidates carry their
   respective enrolled zones. Without the fix, both `candidate.zones`
   vectors were empty and the assertion caught the regression.

## Scope notes

The struct field ordering in the MeshNode constructor places
`local_zones` immediately before `local_symbols` / `local_leases`,
keeping all local-only enrollment state contiguous. PeerState already
had a `zones` field (from the attestation path), so no changes there.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
